explanation: The Sigma rule is specifically looking for flags used by the legacy NT Resource Kit version of auditpol (e.g., /logon:none). Modern Windows versions use a different syntax with '/set', '/subcategory', and ':disable', which performs the same action of impairing logging but does not trigger the specific string matches defined in this rule.

The rule relies on the specific command-line arguments associated with the old Resource Kit utility. By using the built-in modern auditpol.exe with the '/remove /all' flag, an attacker can clear all audit policies without using the '/logon:none' style strings monitored by the rule.

Instead of using the auditpol utility to modify policy, an attacker can directly modify the Registry or use Group Policy objects to disable auditing features. This bypasses the 'process_creation' detection entirely because it avoids the auditpol binary and its specific flags.
